title: guajava costaricensis
intro: Guajava costaricensis, also known as the Costa Rican guava, is a tropical tree species native to Central America, belonging to the family Myrtaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins. The health benefits of Guajava costaricensis include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Guajava costaricensis can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as tea, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
